title: hack/install_golangci.sh: check env vars
body:
Also make it executable and `set -eo pipefail`.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

Based on a comment from @cevich -> https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4870#pullrequestreview-343315780

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

For your own knowledge, this is a bash gotcha (from the man page):

> The  return  status  of  a  pipeline  is  the exit status of the last command, unless the pipefail option is enabled. If pipefail is enabled, the pipeline's return status is the value of the last (rightmost)  command to exit with a non-zero status, or zero if all commands exit successfully.

So my understanding is without pipefail (the default) a ` foo | bar` will give you a zero-exit if `foo` fails but not `bar`.  When pipefail is enabled (not the default), then either `foo` or `bar` failing will result in a non-zero exit.

@edsantiago please correct my understanding, you know this stuff better :smile:
